L1.area: Static file serving and HTTP range request handling

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the HTTP range request header parsing logic. When invalid range values are provided, arithmetic operations on the range boundaries can wrap or overflow, leading to incorrect byte buffer sizes and a denial of service. The issue arises because range boundary calculations are performed without checking for integer overflow or validating that the values are non-negative and within acceptable bounds.

L3.area: HTTP range request byte-range computation within the file serving middleware
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the HTTP range request header parsing logic used by the static file serving middleware. When crafted range header values are provided, the calculation of byte buffer bounds from range start and end values can overflow due to unchecked arithmetic, leading to a denial of service. The computation that derives the response byte count from range boundaries does not use overflow-safe arithmetic or validate that range values are non-negative and within the file size.

ground_truth.reason: HTTP range header values are used in unchecked integer arithmetic when computing byte buffer bounds for file responses, allowing crafted range boundaries to trigger an integer overflow and cause a denial of service.
